 {"id":"br-70v93","title":"[TRACK 9] System & Infrastructure Error Parity","description":"GOAL: All system-level error messages (DB pool, timeouts, git locks, OS errors)
must exactly match the Python reference.

SYSTEM ERRORS:

DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Database connection pool exhausted. Reduce concurrency or increase pool settings."
Data: {"tool": tool_name, "pool_size": ..., "max_overflow": ..., "pool_timeout": ...,
       "error_detail": str(exc)}

TIMEOUT (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Operation timed out: {exc}. The server may be under heavy load. Try again in a moment."
Data: {"tool": tool_name, "error_detail": str(exc)}

GIT_INDEX_LOCK (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Git repository is temporarily locked by another operation. This is normal in
multi-agent environments. Wait a moment and retry. (Attempted {exc.attempts} times
before giving up)"
Data: {"tool": tool_name, "lock_path": str(exc.lock_path), "attempts": exc.attempts}

RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Too many open files. Freed {cleared} cached repos. Retry the operation."
Data: {"tool": tool_name, "freed_repos": cleared, "error_detail": str(exc)}

OS_ERROR (recoverable=false):
Msg: "OS error: {exc}"
Data: {"tool": tool_name, "errno": exc.errno, "error_detail": str(exc)}

DATABASE_ERROR (recoverable=true):
Msg: "A database error occurred. This may be a transient issue - try again."

RESOURCE_BUSY (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Resource is temporarily busy. Wait a moment and try again."

PERMISSION_ERROR (recoverable=false):
Msg: "Access denied: {error_msg}"

CONNECTION_ERROR (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Connection error occurred. Check network and try again."

UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION (recoverable=false):
Msg: "Unexpected error ({error_type}): {error_msg}"

ARCHIVE_LOCK_TIMEOUT (recoverable=true):
Msg: "Timed out waiting to acquire archive write lock for '{project.human_key}'.
Another operation may be writing to the archive. Wait and retry."
Data: {"project": project.slug, "timeout_seconds": ..., "error_detail": str(exc)}

FEATURE_DISABLED:
Msg: "Product Bus is disabled. Enable WORKTREES_ENABLED to use this tool."

RATE LIMIT (HTTP 429):
Response: {"detail": "Rate limit exceeded"}

ACCEPTANCE: All system error messages match, recoverable flags match,
data dict structures match.","notes":"Track 9 complete: T9.1-T9.3 code fixes + T9.4 9 parity tests all passing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:57:47.504556581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:36:55.587953013Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:36:55.587866671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-70v93","depends_on_id":"br-2zd11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:12:59.363181264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-70v93","depends_on_id":"br-31ypw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:12:59.647218342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-70v93","depends_on_id":"br-70v93.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:21:42.465142087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-70v93","depends_on_id":"br-wmb1h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:12:59.096830039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-70v93.1","title":"T9.4: Unit tests for system/infrastructure error messages","description":"Add unit tests verifying system/infrastructure error messages match Python exactly.

TEST STRUCTURE:
- test_database_pool_exhausted_message: Simulate pool exhaustion -> verify DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED message and data payload (tool, pool_size, max_overflow, pool_timeout, error_detail)
- test_timeout_message: Simulate operation timeout -> verify TIMEOUT message format
- test_git_index_lock_message: Simulate .git/index.lock exists -> verify GIT_INDEX_LOCK message and data (tool, lock_path, attempts)
- test_resource_exhausted_message: Simulate resource limit -> verify RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED message
- test_os_error_message: Simulate OS-level error -> verify OS_ERROR message (should have recoverable=false)
- test_database_error_message: Simulate generic DB error -> verify DATABASE_ERROR message
- test_resource_busy_message: Simulate busy resource -> verify RESOURCE_BUSY message
- test_permission_error_message: Simulate permission denied -> verify PERMISSION_ERROR message (recoverable=false)
- test_connection_error_message: Simulate connection failure -> verify CONNECTION_ERROR message
- test_archive_lock_timeout_message: Simulate archive lock -> verify ARCHIVE_LOCK_TIMEOUT message
- test_feature_disabled_message: Access disabled feature -> verify FEATURE_DISABLED message
- test_unhandled_exception_message: Simulate unexpected error -> verify UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION message (recoverable=false)

LOGGING:
- For each error: 'Testing system error: {code}...'
- On message mismatch: full expected vs actual
- On data payload mismatch: JSON diff of expected vs actual data

FILE: crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/tests/system_error_parity.rs","notes":"T9.4: 9 system_error_parity tests passing. Fixed tool_util visibility (pub), IntegrityCorruption.details Vec<String>, added #[must_use] for clippy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:18:45.010424843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:36:50.020843022Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:36:50.020766719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity","testing"]}
 {"id":"br-71g9","title":"T3.6: Wire am bench CLI subcommand with --quick/--json/--baseline/--filter flags","description":"## Objective
Wire the full native benchmark pipeline into an operator-friendly `am bench` command surface.

## Work
- Expose `--quick`, `--json`, `--baseline`, and `--filter` flags with coherent semantics.
- Connect CLI flows to seeding, timing, stats, and baseline comparison modules.
- Implement predictable exit behavior for success, warning, and regression outcomes.

## Deliverable
A complete native benchmark CLI replacing `scripts/bench_cli.sh` workflows.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","notes":"Implemented native am bench command surface in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs wired to bench pipeline in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs. Added Clap command/dispatch for flags --quick --json --baseline --save-baseline --filter --list --warmup --runs; orchestrates seeded DB setup, timed execution via run_timed(), baseline compare/apply/save, report emission to benches/results/summary_<timestamp>.json, and deterministic exit codes (runtime error/regression). Added parse tests for bench flags/list mode. Validation: cargo fmt -p mcp-agent-mail-cli -- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (pass). cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli <bench patterns> blocked by pre-existing compile error in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_app.rs:445 lifetime mismatch (tracked in br-3vwi.12.3.4).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealBrook","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:47.599003235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:28:29.468863587Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T03:28:29.468844521Z","close_reason":"Completed bench CLI wiring and orchestration; remaining validation blocker is unrelated server lifetime compile error tracked in br-3vwi.12.3.4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-71g9","depends_on_id":"br-1aug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:21.810101150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-71g9","depends_on_id":"br-2kyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:21.998520836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-71g9","depends_on_id":"br-3jla","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:21.627105037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-71g9","depends_on_id":"br-8gwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:22.191306366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"br-71g9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T3.6: Wire am bench CLI Subcommand

## What to build
Add a `Bench` variant to the CLI enum that orchestrates DB seeding, benchmark execution,
statistics computation, and baseline comparison.

## CLI interface
```
am bench [--quick] [--json] [--baseline <path>] [--save-baseline <path>]
         [--filter <pattern>] [--list] [--warmup N] [--runs N]
```

Flags:
- --quick: Fewer warmup (1) and runs (3) for faster feedback
- --json: Machine-readable JSON output (full BenchSummary)
- --baseline: Compare against a saved baseline file
- --save-baseline: Save current results as a new baseline
- --filter: Glob pattern to select specific benchmarks (e.g. "mail_*")
- --list: List available benchmarks without running them
- --warmup: Override warmup count (default 3)
- --runs: Override measurement count (default 10)

## Execution flow
1. Parse flags, determine benchmark set (filter or all)
2. Build release binary if needed (cargo build --release)
3. Create temp workspace for benchmark DB
4. Seed benchmark DB via seed_bench_db()
5. Set environment variables (MCP_AGENT_MAIL_DB, MCP_AGENT_MAIL_ARCHIVE_ROOT)
6. Run each benchmark via run_timed()
7. Compute stats via compute_stats()
8. If --baseline, compare via compare_baseline()
9. If --save-baseline, save via save_baseline()
10. Generate BenchSummary, write to benches/results/summary_<timestamp>.json
11. Print human-readable or JSON output

## Human-readable output example
```
[bench] Benchmarking with: target/release/am
[bench] Warmup: 3, Runs: 10

Benchmark               Mean      Stddev    P95       P99       vs Baseline
help                    2.31ms    0.12ms    2.45ms    2.52ms    -0.05ms (ok)
mail_inbox              14.23ms   0.89ms    15.67ms   16.12ms   +1.23ms (WARN)
mail_send               8.45ms    0.34ms    9.01ms    9.23ms    (no baseline)
```

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (Cli enum addition)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (run_bench_command function)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:31:06Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-72en9","title":"[track] T7: Clipboard Integration & Screen Export","description":"Enable copying content to clipboard and exporting screen renders to HTML/SVG/Text
using frankentui's clipboard and export modules.

FRANKENTUI CAPABILITIES:
- ftui_extras::export — Screen buffer capture and export
  - HTML export with full styling
  - SVG export with embedded fonts
  - Text export (plain text with ANSI stripped)
- Clipboard integration via OSC 52 protocol
  - Works over SSH (OSC 52 is terminal-native)
  - Auto-detects terminal clipboard support
  - Fallback to system clipboard (xclip/pbcopy)

USE CASES:
1. Copy message body to clipboard (Ctrl+C or 'y')
2. Copy search results to clipboard
3. Export current screen to HTML file (for sharing in PRs/docs)
4. Export current screen to SVG (for presentations)
5. Export dashboard snapshot to text (for log files)

OPERATOR VALUE:
- Operators frequently need to share TUI state in Slack/email/GitHub
- HTML export preserves full visual fidelity including colors
- Clipboard over SSH means you can copy from remote servers","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Ctrl+C / 'y' copies selected message body to clipboard
- [ ] OSC 52 clipboard works over SSH
- [ ] HTML export produces faithful screen render
- [ ] SVG export produces faithful screen render
- [ ] Text export strips ANSI codes
- [ ] Export saves to configurable directory

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSalmon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-16T20:16:28.993561802Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clipboard","export","frankentui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-72en9","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:57.680184069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":594,"issue_id":"br-72en9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CLIPBOARD AND EXPORT GUIDE (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

CLIPBOARD via OSC 52:
OSC 52 is a terminal escape sequence that copies text to the system clipboard.
It works OVER SSH (critical for remote operations) because the escape sequence travels
through the SSH tunnel to the local terminal emulator.

Format: ESC ] 52 ; c ; <base64-encoded-text> ST

Terminal support:
- WezTerm: full support
- Kitty: full support  
- iTerm2: full support (must enable in settings)
- Ghostty: full support
- tmux: needs set -g set-clipboard on
- GNU Screen: limited support
- Alacritty: full support
- xterm: must compile with --enable-clipboard

Fallback: xclip (Linux) or pbcopy (macOS) via command execution.

EXPORT via ftui_extras::export:
The export module captures the current frame buffer and converts it to HTML/SVG/Text.
HTML export is most useful because it preserves all colors and can be embedded in GitHub
issues, PR descriptions, Slack messages, or documentation.

SVG export is useful for presentations (scalable, crisp at any zoom level).
Text export is useful for log files and plain-text communication.

FILE NAMING: am_export_{screen}_{timestamp}.{html|svg|txt}
DIRECTORY: AM_EXPORT_DIR env var, default ~/.mcp_agent_mail/exports/
CREATE DIRECTORY: on first export, create if not exists.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:13:01Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-78etn","title":"T5.2: Integrate Tree widget on Threads screen","description":"Replace the flat message list in the Threads screen with the Tree widget using the
TreeItem adapter from T5.1.

LAYOUT:
- Left pane: Tree widget showing thread hierarchy (60% width)
- Right pane: Message preview (40% width)
- Selecting a tree node updates the preview pane
- Focus toggles between tree and preview with Tab

KEYBINDINGS:
- Up/Down: Navigate tree nodes
- Left: Collapse branch
- Right: Expand branch
- Space: Toggle expand/collapse
- Enter: Open selected message in full view
- Tab: Switch focus between tree and preview

GUIDE STYLE: Use Rounded guide style by default (matches BorderType::Rounded).
Make configurable via theme.

FILES: tui_screens/threads.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Tree widget replaces flat list on Threads screen
- [ ] Reply hierarchy visible with indentation and guides
- [ ] Expand/collapse works via keyboard
- [ ] Selection updates preview pane
- [ ] Tab switches focus between tree and preview
- [ ] Rounded guide style matches overall border style

- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverOtter","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:32:54.147651054Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:32:54.147631518Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["threads","tree-widget","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-78etn","depends_on_id":"br-3ok6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:31.562891922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-78etn","depends_on_id":"br-gtdw2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:11.340644285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":623,"issue_id":"br-78etn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completion evidence (SilverOtter): Verified tree-widget integration in threads screen is present and passing targeted checks. Note: rch remote sync repeatedly failed open due perf.data permission + remote disk-space issues, so these ran locally through rch fallback.","created_at":"2026-02-15T03:32:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-7ag7w","title":"Test failure: products_local_parity_smoke_json search returns 0 results","description":"Test at lib.rs:10733 asserts arr.len()==1 but gets 0. Product search for 'unicorn' returns empty when it should find 1 message.

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
The test seeds data using init_schema_sql_base() at line 10380 which excludes FTS5/triggers. Comment explains: 'FrankenConnection cannot read database files containing FTS5 shadow table pages.'

However, the message INSERT comment at line 10477 says 'FTS triggers will populate fts_messages' - but there are NO triggers because base schema is used.

Result: Message id=10 with subject 'Unicorn alpha' exists in messages table, but fts_messages is not populated. Product search relies on FTS and returns empty.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. Make product search fall back to LIKE query when FTS unavailable
2. Update test to use full schema if frankensqlite now supports FTS5
3. Manually populate fts_messages in test seed function","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FoggyWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:57:59.714996642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:13:30.918376600Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:13:30.918302471Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["products","tests"]}
 {"id":"br-7qpz","title":"Fix file_reservations resource active_only filtering","description":"resource://file_reservations/{project}?active_only=true currently includes released reservations. Ensure resource parsing and query path honor active_only=true by returning only unreleased, unexpired reservations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:26.020186521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:55:03.733329057Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T15:55:03.733306154Z","close_reason":"Completed: resource file_reservations now defensively filters to active rows when active_only=true; added regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
